title: After a Fall: A First-Week Recovery Plan
summary: Simple steps for appointments, mobility support, and household adjustments after an injury.
body: Day 1 starts with safety and communication. Call your primary care office or urgent care line, explain what happened, and ask whether you need imaging, a same-day visit, or emergency care.
Write down your pain level, dizziness, and mobility in a notebook morning and evening. Clinicians can make better decisions when they can see clear trends instead of one vague description.
If you live alone, set up a simple check-in plan for the next seven days. One call in the morning and one in the evening can prevent a small setback from becoming a crisis.
Ask for a home safety review focused on rugs, cluttered walkways, stair lighting, and bathroom support points. Remove one hazard per room this week instead of trying to fix everything at once.
Keep essentials at waist level to avoid bending and reaching. Move medications, phone chargers, water, and frequently used kitchen items to easy-to-reach locations.
Schedule help for groceries, rides, and medication pickup before you need it. Recovery goes better when logistics are handled early rather than after you are already exhausted.
At your follow-up appointment, ask three specific questions: what activities are safe now, what warning signs need urgent care, and what level of pain is expected versus concerning.
Before the week ends, build a two-week plan: follow-up dates, transportation, and who to call if pain, swelling, or confusion suddenly worsens.

seo.title: Injury Recovery Guidance for Retirees
seo.description: Plain-language first-week plan after a fall or mobility injury.
